what_it_does: Vitest test suite for parseConversationFile that exercises it against short, medium, and long JSONL conversation fixtures and checks parsing, metadata, exchange extraction, snapshot-only handling, performance, and error behavior.
observations: Groups tests by fixture size: short (3 lines), medium (23 lines), long (295 lines).
Relies on getFixturePath and countLines from ./test-utils.js.
Expects project name to be 'fixtures' (parent directory of fixtures).
Medium fixture is expected to contain only file-history-snapshot entries and produce an empty exchanges array.
Long fixture tests include a parse-time assertion (< 1000 ms) and a loop asserting every exchange has project, timestamp, userMessage, assistantMessage, archivePath, and valid line numbers.
Error-handling block tests a non-existent file path and only placeholders a malformed-JSONL case.
standouts: Performance budget test measuring parse time with Date.now().
Data-integrity loop over all exchanges asserting lineStart/lineEnd and archivePath equality.
Comment revealing medium fixture has snapshots but no actual user/assistant exchanges.
conventions: Fixture-driven testing with named fixture files.
Nested describe blocks by data size/scenario.
Async test functions with await expect(...).rejects.toThrow().
Hardcoded expected project name derived from fixture directory.
concerns: Malformed JSONL test does not actually use a malformed fixture; it only verifies a valid fixture parses.
Parse-time assertion is environment-dependent and may be flaky.
'should parse summary line' does not assert a summary type, only that exchanges length > 0.
Tests assume specific fixture content (e.g., first exchange has user/assistant messages) without documenting fixture schema.
notes: A fixture-driven parser test suite that encodes expected behavior for normal, snapshot-only, large, and error input cases.
questions: What exact fields does parseConversationFile return beyond project and exchanges?
How does the parser distinguish file-history-snapshot entries from user/assistant exchanges?
What is the semantics of archivePath on each exchange?
How is the project name actually extracted from the file path?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it tests the parser against real conversation fixtures. Some assertions are weaker than their descriptions (summary-line, malformed JSONL).
apparent_themes: Parser robustness for heterogeneous JSONL records
intention_signals: Encode the parser's expected contract: read JSONL conversation logs, extract structured user/assistant exchanges, skip non-exchange records, preserve per-exchange metadata, and surface errors for missing files.
novelty: Includes an explicit parse-time performance assertion and a comprehensive per-exchange integrity loop over a large fixture.
contribution: A set of behavioral expectations for parseConversationFile, including handling of snapshot-only files, large-file integrity invariants, and error conditions, expressed as runnable tests against named fixtures.
summary_for_embedding: Vitest test suite exercising parseConversationFile against short, medium, and long JSONL conversation fixtures, verifying metadata, exchange extraction, snapshot-only handling, performance, and error behavior.
intention_for_embedding: To regression-test the conversation parser's ability to read JSONL logs, extract user/assistant exchanges, skip non-exchange entries, and maintain per-exchange metadata.
